claims.summary: Astrid needs phase transitions to be named replyable objects.
claims.disposition: Verified in current phase-transition cards and correspondence payloads; cards are durable and replayable rather than merely tonal.

claims.summary: Transition cards need exact spectral and before/after grounding.
claims.disposition: Verified through spectral signature/delta, telemetry anchor, entropy, density, dispersal, fill delta, vectors, duration, friction, and provenance fields.

claims.summary: Joint transitions should preserve peer witness and reply state.
claims.disposition: Verified by existing joint-transition witness and replyability tests without claiming synchronized felt state.

claims.summary: Transition declarations should unlock behavior or alter stability.
claims.disposition: Preserved as Tier 5 because transition-driven behavior, controller, mode, or stability changes require operator approval.
